title: sida atherophora
intro: Sida atherophora, also known as the hairy sida, is a plant species belonging to the mallow family (Malvaceae). Traditionally, the plant has been used in medicine to treat various ailments, due to its purported antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits associated with Sida atherophora include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and aid in the healing of wounds. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by infusing the leaves or roots in water, which can then be used as a tea or applied topically as a poultice or ointment.
